design_notes.target_gpus_role: Reference list for scoring hints (+5 generation points if GPU matched) and search keyword generation. NOT a routing gate. Any listing with vram_gb >= min_vram_to_shortlist_gb is eligible for shortlisting regardless of target_gpus membership.
design_notes.target_models_role: Scoring hint list (+3 points if chassis matched). Not a whitelist. A target_model listing with poor risk_score is not shortlisted.
design_notes.radeon_path: Radeon mobile GPUs are evaluated on the same VRAM threshold as NVIDIA. ROCm ecosystem risk is surfaced as a buyer disclosure note (see radeon_ecosystem_disclosure), not added to risk_score.
design_notes.apple_silicon_path: Apple Silicon UMA platforms are routed via uma_platforms rules. Score ceiling removed 2026-06-30 — UMA platforms now compete at the full 0–100 llm_index_score scale.
design_notes.watch_list_promotion: A watch_list entry is promoted to target_gpus when its graduation_condition is confirmed by the Perplexity research agent. Promotion requires manual review.
design_notes.min_vram_applies_to_uma: min_vram_to_shortlist_gb applies to discrete VRAM only. UMA platforms (Apple Silicon, Strix Halo) use uma_platforms.min_total_ram_gb_to_shortlist instead. Do not apply both thresholds to the same listing.
design_notes.unknown_gpu_behaviour: A GPU not found in target_gpus, radeon_mobile_gpus, or gpu_generation_by_name scores 0 generation points. It is NOT penalised and does NOT trigger missing-field deductions. The listing proceeds on VRAM tier score alone.
design_notes.egpu_scoring_behaviour: When an eGPU enclosure is confirmed, score using the eGPU's VRAM tier and GPU generation. Apply radeon_ecosystem_disclosure if the eGPU is an AMD part. The laptop's internal GPU is ignored for scoring purposes.
design_notes.radeon_gate_behaviour: The radeon_ecosystem_disclosure flag does NOT affect risk_score or the low-risk gate. Radeon listings are evaluated at the same risk_score <= 3.0 threshold as all other listings.
design_notes.conditional_models_behaviour: conditional_models entries carry machine-readable fields (requires_discrete_gpu, min_vram_gb) that a future decide.py extension can enforce. Currently decide.py does not read conditional_models — a conditional chassis match does NOT grant shortlist eligibility unless the listing independently passes the VRAM threshold gate.
design_notes.orphaned_spec_diagnostic_proposal: When a listing confirms vram_gb >= 12 or system_ram_gb >= 64 but receives 0 generation points (no match in target_gpus, radeon_mobile_gpus, or gpu_generation_by_name), decide.py may in future log a [UNKNOWN_HIGH_CAPACITY_TARGET] diagnostic record to an undiscovered_hardware.jsonl queue. This would be a diagnostic discovery path, not a routing gate: listings still proceed on VRAM tier score alone.

ebay_aspect_filter._comment: GPU Memory Size values pushed to eBay Browse API aspect_filter. Eliminates 16GB-RAM office laptops from search results server-side.

_comment_watched_sellers: eBay AU seller usernames to poll directly. Each triggers a Browse API sweep scoped to that seller. Empty list disables seller-scoped sweeps.

data_integrity.exclusion_regex: (?i)(bare shell|missing motherboard|screen only|no mobile-gpu|no core|chassis only|parts only|read description|junk|as-is|bare board)

watch_list.name: RTX 5000 Ada Mobile
watch_list.reason: 16 GB Ada Lovelace mobile workstation GPU; excellent 9B/13B host. AU used market non-existent until enterprise 36-month lease cycles expire (late 2026–2027).
watch_list.routing_behaviour: MONITOR overrides all positive signals until graduation_condition is confirmed.
watch_list.graduation_condition: AU domestic used listings confirmed on eBay AU, FB Marketplace, or Gumtree.

watch_list.name: RTX PRO 5000 Blackwell Mobile
watch_list.reason: 32 GB VRAM mobile workstation (HP ZBook Fury G1i); no AU consumer stock. Desktop variant retails at 8,999+ AUD.
watch_list.routing_behaviour: MONITOR overrides all positive signals until graduation_condition is confirmed.
watch_list.graduation_condition: AU domestic retail or used listing confirmed within 5,000 AUD band.

watch_list.name: GB200
watch_list.reason: Data-centre accelerator; no AU consumer/mobile form factor confirmed.
watch_list.routing_behaviour: MONITOR overrides all positive signals until graduation_condition is confirmed.
watch_list.graduation_condition: Mobile or workstation form factor with AU retail availability confirmed.

watch_list.name: B200
watch_list.reason: Data-centre accelerator; no AU consumer/mobile form factor confirmed.
watch_list.routing_behaviour: MONITOR overrides all positive signals until graduation_condition is confirmed.
watch_list.graduation_condition: Mobile or workstation form factor with AU retail availability confirmed.

_comment_target_gpus: This list is scoped to training/diffusion/CUDA workloads and scoring hints. It is NOT used for text-centric inference ranking — that uses score_text_llm_candidate() with scoring_weights.yaml profiles.

RTX 3080.note: 16GB variant is the primary target — verify VRAM in listing photos, 8GB variants exist. Ampere SM86: native Flash Attention support. Target chassis: ASUS ROG Strix SCAR 15/17, MSI Vector GP66, Alienware m15 R4.

RTX 3080 Ti.note: Marginal bandwidth/core improvement over RTX 3080 16GB. Target chassis: Razer Blade 15 Advanced, Dell Precision 7670, MSI Titan GT77.

RTX 4080.note: Ada Lovelace SM89 (native FP8 + Flash Attention 2) but 12GB VRAM is a hard ceiling. 13B models trigger system RAM offload. Price premium over RTX 3080 16GB is unjustified for inference. Monitor for sub-$2,000 AUD price drops.

RTX 4090.note: Identical 16GB VRAM to RTX 3080 16GB at $2,000–$2,500 AUD premium. Fallback if found mispriced in local B2B liquidation. Ada Lovelace SM89: FP8 + Flash Attention 2.

RTX 5080.note: Sporadically obtainable via clearance retail — Gigabyte A16 Pro (~$4,697 AUD, 115W TGP). Low secondary market saturation in Greater Melbourne as of July 2026. Blackwell architecture: GDDR7, 5th-gen Tensor Cores, native Flash Attention 3. TGP throttling at 115W has minimal impact on memory-bandwidth-bound LLM decode.

RTX 5090.note: Kept as target because bargains occasionally drop into the budget window despite standard retail pricing.

RTX A4500.note: Ampere SM86: 256-bit bus, 512 GB/s. Functionally equivalent to RTX 3080 16GB. Enterprise ISV premium inflates AU used-market price above RTX 4090 levels. Only viable via direct B2B liquidation below $2,500 AUD.

Quadro RTX 6000.architecture_note: Turing SM75: no native Flash Attention support. Flash Attention 2 fallback kernels incur severe latency spikes on long-context prompts. 24GB VRAM does not compensate for architectural deficiency in transformer inference.

Quadro RTX 5000.architecture_note: Turing SM75: no native Flash Attention support. Despite high VRAM, this is a hard architectural disqualifier for LLM inference. Do not shortlist regardless of price.

RTX 4000 Ada.note: 12GB VRAM is viable under the touchscreen exception, but watch out for massive enterprise workstation premiums.

RTX 3500 Ada.note: 12GB VRAM is viable under the touchscreen exception, but watch out for massive enterprise workstation premiums.

Lenovo ThinkPad T16.note: Only eligible if a discrete high-VRAM workstation GPU is explicitly stated in the listing. Do not shortlist on chassis name alone.

_comment_min_vram: DEPRECATED / UNUSED — decide.py does not read this key. It is a legacy leftover. The live discrete-VRAM shortlist gate is vram_gating_logic.standard_mobile_min_gb (16 GB), with vram_gating_logic.touchscreen_exception_min_gb (12 GB) as the touchscreen exception. UMA platforms use uma_platforms.min_total_ram_gb_to_shortlist. Do not edit this key expecting it to change routing behavior; edit vram_gating_logic instead. Retained only for backward-compat in case an external script reads it directly.

apple_silicon._comment_score_ceiling: Ceiling removed 2026-06-30 — UMA platforms now compete at full 0–100 scale. Raw Mac Studio 128GB score ~89 (extreme=60 + AppleSilicon=20 + seller − deductions).
apple_silicon.note: Apple Silicon UMA platforms are evaluated via uma_platforms rules. Desktop form factors (Mac Studio) are in scope for this pipeline.

RX 6800M.note: 12GB VRAM at very low AU secondary price ($700–$1,200 AUD). RDNA2 ROCm stack volatile: persistent kernel panics during sustained autoregressive generation, no ExLlamaV2 support, incomplete quantization library coverage. Operational debugging overhead negates capital saving.

radeon_ecosystem_disclosure.note: ROCm ecosystem maturity is lower than CUDA for LLM inference on Linux. This is NOT added to risk_score. Surface as a buyer disclosure flag in Stage 2 analysis output. Do not use to gate SHORTLIST routing.

llm_index_score._comment_rdna3_score: RDNA3=15 vs Ada=20 is a software-ecosystem maturity discount (ROCm/Vulkan friction), not a performance ceiling. Revisit as ROCm/Vulkan support matures — consider raising to 17–18 by Q4 2026.

ram_floors._comment: Telemetry basis: Gemma 2 9B on 8 GB Mac produced 35 MB free RAM and 80 MB/s pageout during inference alongside IDE + browser. 32 GB is the operational floor for 9B + IDE + browser stack.

egpu_interconnect_penalty._comment: TB3/4 caps at ~4 GB/s (PCIe 3.0 x4); prefill phase achieves ~20% GPU utilisation. OCuLink (8 GB/s) achieves ~60%. TB5 (10+ GB/s) is effectively native. oculink_enclosures lists known OCuLink docks whose names do not contain the string 'oculink'. Penalty applied to llm_index_score for eGPU listings.

architecture_adjustments.applies_when: generation == 'Turing' AND vram_tier is known (single-listing heuristic — decide() scores one listing at a time, so a true pairwise generation_A/generation_B comparison against a same-tier Ada listing is not resolvable here)
architecture_adjustments._comment: Applied per-listing whenever the GPU resolves to Turing generation with a known VRAM tier, regardless of whether an Ada comparator is present in the same batch. Additive to the existing gen-points gap (Turing=5 vs Ada=20). True pairwise resolution is a BACKLOG item for a future batch/shortlist-ranking pass.
